name: Ripsaw
description: Four-voice supersaw oscillator on the HEX platform · 7 saw-to-square morphing oscillators per voice (28 total, 56 with stereo spread) · Shape, Spread, Shift CV over hard-sync-style detune · stereo L/R with voice detection on insertion.

controls.name: Encoder
controls.description: Turn to step all four voices in semitones; short press to access cents fine-tune (0–99); long press to reset to C2.
controls.detail: turn: ±semitones · short press: cents 0-99 · long press: reset to C2 (65.41Hz)

controls.name: Shape
controls.description: Morphs every oscillator's waveform from saw to square through continuous blend. Applies equally across all 28 oscillators.
controls.detail: CCW: saw · CW: square · continuous morph

controls.name: Spread
controls.description: Detunes the 7 oscillators per voice using classic 1990s supersaw offset curves. Controls the swarm width from unison to fully detuned chorus.
controls.detail: CCW: unison · CW: wide swarm · curves modelled on JP-8000

controls.name: Shift
controls.description: Raises the pitch of 28 per-voice hard-sync oscillators, producing supersync formant sweeps.
controls.detail: CCW: no sync · CW: rising sync · responds at audio rate via Shift CV

controls.name: Fine tune (encoder short-press)
controls.description: Cents fine-tune with optional semitone quantization from the menu.

controls.name: LED screen
controls.description: Displays encoder value, current musical note and menu state.

inputs.name: V/O 1
inputs.description: 1V/oct pitch CV for voice 1. Voice 1 is always active. Range tracks C0 to C7.

inputs.name: V/O 2
inputs.description: 1V/oct pitch CV for voice 2. Inserting a cable activates voice 2 (voice detection) and adds 7 more oscillators to the stereo field.

inputs.name: V/O 3
inputs.description: 1V/oct pitch CV for voice 3. Cable insertion activates the voice.

inputs.name: V/O 4
inputs.description: 1V/oct pitch CV for voice 4. Cable insertion activates the voice — full four-voice paraphony at 56 oscillators across L/R.

inputs.name: Shape CV
inputs.description: Modulates Shape parameter. Responsive up to roughly 500Hz so audio-rate PWM-style sweeps are possible.

inputs.name: Spread CV
inputs.description: Modulates Spread. Swarm opens and closes under CV. Audio-rate safe up to ~500Hz.

inputs.name: Shift CV
inputs.description: Modulates Shift, giving CV control over the hard-sync offset for sync-sweep formant motion.

outputs.name: Out L
outputs.description: Left channel. Each voice's 7 oscillators are spread across L and R so the swarm fills the stereo field rather than stacking mono.

outputs.name: Out R
outputs.description: Right channel. Complement of Out L; patch both for a wide stereo image or either alone for a mono sum of the swarm.

behaviors.name: JP-8000 style supersaw swarm
behaviors.trigger: default patch with Shape CCW, Spread at taste
behaviors.description: Seven detuned saws per voice using the same offset curves as Roland's JP-8000 supersaw produce the iconic trance-lead swarm. Spread controls width from unison to chorus-heavy without reducing level.

behaviors.name: Voice detection by cable insertion
behaviors.description: Ripsaw adds voices only when their V/O jacks receive cables. One voice = 7 oscillators; four voices = 28 oscillators, doubled across stereo for 56. Paraphony scales with patching.

behaviors.name: Shape as saw-square morph
behaviors.description: Blends every oscillator continuously from sawtooth to square. Mid positions sit in unusual asymmetric territory. Shape CV is audio-rate capable for PWM-style motion across the whole swarm.

behaviors.name: Supersync formant sweep
behaviors.trigger: Shift knob raised above zero or Shift CV modulated
behaviors.description: Raises the pitch of a per-oscillator hard-sync master by a ratio while the slaves keep the original pitch. Classic hard-sync formant whine, but multiplied across 28 oscillators — hence 'supersync'.

behaviors.name: Semitone-quantized fine tune
behaviors.trigger: menu option · short-press encoder for cents
behaviors.description: Fine-tune ring can be locked to semitones for chord-tuned paraphonic voices or left free for cents-level detune offsets. Root pitch stays set via encoder; V/O inputs transpose from there.

behaviors.name: Stereo oscillator spread
behaviors.trigger: patching Out L and Out R
behaviors.description: Each voice's 7 oscillators alternate L / R so a single voice already images wide. Summing to mono cancels some of the wow and fatness — keep stereo downstream to preserve the supersaw character.

behaviors.name: HEX firmware platform
behaviors.description: Ripsaw is the first firmware for Acid Rain's 6HP HEX platform. Additional free firmware images reflash the same hardware into different modules (granular, delay, etc.), so the panel engraving names the current firmware rather than fixed function.

patchIdeas.name: Classic supersaw trance lead
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer p> V/O 1 · Shape CCW for saw · Spread 2 o'clock · Shift 0 · Out L/R → stereo delay → reverb · one voice already delivers 7 detuned saws wide in stereo · move Spread by LFO for shimmer
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripsaw:
* Shape: CCW (saw)
* Spread: 2 o'clock
* Shift: 0

Sequencer:
LFO:
FX Stereo:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Ripsaw (V/O 1)
- LFO (Out) >> Ripsaw (Spread CV)
- Ripsaw (Out L) -> FX Stereo (In L)
- Ripsaw (Out R) -> FX Stereo (In R)

patchIdeas.name: Four-voice paraphonic chord
patchIdeas.patch: Poly quantizer or chord module → V/O 1-4 · insertion activates all four voices = 28 oscillators (56 stereo) · root held by encoder · single trigger gates a stereo pad through an external envelope/VCA · big cinematic stacks from 6HP
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripsaw:
* Spread: mid
* Shape: 11 o'clock
* Shift: 0

Chord Module:
Envelope:
Stereo VCA:

- Chord Module (Out 1) p> Ripsaw (V/O 1)
- Chord Module (Out 2) p> Ripsaw (V/O 2)
- Chord Module (Out 3) p> Ripsaw (V/O 3)
- Chord Module (Out 4) p> Ripsaw (V/O 4)
- Ripsaw (Out L) -> Stereo VCA (In L)
- Ripsaw (Out R) -> Stereo VCA (In R)
- Envelope (Out) >> Stereo VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Supersync sweep lead
patchIdeas.patch: V/O 1 from melody sequencer · slow envelope → Shift CV for rising sync-formant sweep each note · Spread moderate · Shape towards square for more harmonics before sync · hard-sync whine multiplied across 28 oscillators
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripsaw:
* Shape: 2 o'clock (more square)
* Spread: noon
* Shift: 0 (CV-driven)

Sequencer:
Envelope:
Clock:
Output:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Ripsaw (V/O 1)
- Clock (Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> Ripsaw (Shift CV)
- Ripsaw (Out L) -> Output (L)
- Ripsaw (Out R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Audio-rate PWM-style Shape FM
patchIdeas.patch: Second VCO square at audio rate → Shape CV · Shape CV responsive to ~500Hz so it behaves like PWM across every one of the 28 saws · adds brightness and sideband content to the swarm without a wavefolder · Spread sets width
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripsaw:
* Shape: noon
* Spread: 2 o'clock

VCO Mod:
Sequencer:
Output:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Ripsaw (V/O 1)
- VCO Mod (Out) >> Ripsaw (Shape CV)
- Ripsaw (Out L) -> Output (L)
- Ripsaw (Out R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Unison bass through a filter
patchIdeas.patch: Spread fully CCW = near unison · Shape 11 o'clock · sub-bass melody on V/O 1 only (other voices off) · Out L → mono low-pass filter → VCA · fat seven-oscillator unison bass with micro-detune chorus — not a thin single saw
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripsaw:
* Shape: 11 o'clock
* Spread: fully CCW
* Shift: 0

Sequencer:
Filter:
VCA:
Envelope:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Ripsaw (V/O 1)
- Ripsaw (Out L) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo drone with modulated Spread and Shift
patchIdeas.patch: Hold a long chord on V/O 1-2 · slow LFO → Spread CV (wide/narrow breathing) · slow random → Shift CV (wandering sync formant) · Out L/R into stereo reverb · evolving drone where internal motion replaces LFOs on filter cutoff
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripsaw:
* Shape: noon
* Spread: CV-driven
* Shift: CV-driven

Chord:
LFO:
Random:
Reverb:

- Chord (Out 1) p> Ripsaw (V/O 1)
- Chord (Out 2) p> Ripsaw (V/O 2)
- LFO (Out) >> Ripsaw (Spread CV)
- Random (Out) >> Ripsaw (Shift CV)
- Ripsaw (Out L) -> Reverb (In L)
- Ripsaw (Out R) -> Reverb (In R)

patchIdeas.name: Percussive pluck with Shift envelope
patchIdeas.patch: Short AD envelope → Shift CV for 'pew' formant transient · second short envelope → VCA · Shape CW for square-ish bite · each note gets a hard-sync chirp before settling into swarm · stereo pluck in 6HP
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ripsaw:
* Shape: 3 o'clock
* Spread: 1 o'clock

Clock:
Envelope Shift:
Envelope Amp:
VCA Stereo:
Sequencer:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Ripsaw (V/O 1)
- Clock (Out) t> Envelope Shift (Trig)
- Clock (Out) t> Envelope Amp (Trig)
- Envelope Shift (Out) >> Ripsaw (Shift CV)
- Ripsaw (Out L) -> VCA Stereo (In L)
- Ripsaw (Out R) -> VCA Stereo (In R)
- Envelope Amp (Out) >> VCA Stereo (CV)
